Summary: Fix provider publication/listing PG durability parity
Description: Replace in-memory publication/listing writes on beta-critical paths with Postgres-backed persistence and deterministic read models.
Acceptance Criteria: Provider publication and listing state survives process restart; parity tests pass in memory and pg modes; no behavior drift on existing API contracts.

Summary: Close finance reconciliation PG parity gap
Description: Align reconciliation and triage read/write paths so production PG mode has full feature parity with local mode.
Acceptance Criteria: Reconciliation triage endpoints return complete data in PG mode; regression tests cover mismatch scenarios and fail closed on missing evidence.

Summary: Add x402 webhook upsert op-kind parity
Description: Implement missing `X402_WEBHOOK_ENDPOINT_UPSERT` op-kind support across storage adapters and validation paths.
Acceptance Criteria: Webhook endpoint upsert succeeds in supported environments; op-kind validation is consistent across adapters; negative tests assert deterministic reject codes.

Summary: Remove ops token query-param auth path
Description: Delete query-parameter auth fallback and require header/session-based auth for all ops surfaces.
Acceptance Criteria: All ops endpoints reject query-param token auth; audit log verifies header/session auth only; compatibility note published with migration guidance.

Summary: Enforce single release version source of truth
Description: Bind `package.json` and `NOOTERRA_VERSION` through a gate that blocks release on mismatch.
Acceptance Criteria: Release checks fail when versions differ; release artifact embeds a single canonical version string; changelog references same version.

Summary: Harden external conformance binary resolution
Description: Make conformance commands resolve runtime binaries deterministically for external users without hidden local assumptions.
Acceptance Criteria: Conformance commands run from fresh external install; path resolution errors emit actionable, stable diagnostics; CI includes external-path smoke.

Summary: Stabilize auth-mode and signup/login contract
Description: Freeze and test public auth-mode discovery and tenant login/signup contract behavior across enabled modes.
Acceptance Criteria: Auth-mode endpoint and login/signup routes pass contract tests for enabled modes; disabled modes fail closed with stable reason codes.

Summary: Enforce tenant-scoped RBAC on ops routes
Description: Apply explicit tenant and role checks across operational endpoints used by onboarding and support.
Acceptance Criteria: Unauthorized role access is denied on every guarded route; cross-tenant access attempts fail deterministically and are audit logged.

Summary: Add OTP/session replay protections
Description: Add nonce, expiry, and replay detection to OTP/session flows with explicit machine-readable reject reasons.
Acceptance Criteria: Expired/replayed OTP/session tokens are rejected with stable reason codes; success path unaffected; tests cover clock skew boundaries.

Summary: Automate key rotation and revoke drill
Description: Codify and test key rotation plus revoke workflows with rollback-safe runbook steps.
Acceptance Criteria: Rotation and revoke drills pass in staging; previously issued artifacts remain verifiable; emergency rollback path is documented and tested.

Summary: Stabilize `nooterra agent init` scaffold output
Description: Ensure scaffolded projects include runnable policy, tests, and manifest defaults that pass first-run smoke.
Acceptance Criteria: Fresh scaffold completes install and local run without manual edits; generated tests pass; scaffold manifest is schema-valid.

Summary: Publish official JS SDK beta channel
Description: Ship JS SDK with pinned examples for first verified run and stable semantic versioning.
Acceptance Criteria: SDK package is published and installable; first-run example succeeds against staging; version tags map to release artifacts.

Summary: Publish provider-kit package and template
Description: Package provider-tool starter with deterministic defaults and compatibility checks.
Acceptance Criteria: Provider kit installs from npm and generates a working paid-tool provider; conformance baseline passes on generated project.

Summary: Add external install matrix CI gate
Description: Gate release on clean external install matrix for `npm`, `npx`, python SDK smoke, and CLI command surface.
Acceptance Criteria: CI matrix passes in clean environments; failures include direct remediation hints; release is blocked if any install lane regresses.

Summary: Add agent build pipeline smoke test
Description: Add CI flow that scaffolds an agent, runs it, executes an intent/work-order cycle, and verifies settlement retrieval.
Acceptance Criteria: Pipeline smoke succeeds on main branch; run emits replay-pack and settlement evidence links; failures are deterministic and triaged.

Summary: Freeze beta onboarding API profile
Description: Freeze the beta onboarding route profile and publish compatibility constraints for clients.
Acceptance Criteria: Beta onboarding profile doc is published with route list and required fields; API contract tests enforce compatibility.

Summary: Implement deterministic onboarding step journal
Description: Track onboarding progression with step-level machine-readable outcomes and deterministic failure categories.
Acceptance Criteria: Onboarding journal output includes stable step IDs and outcomes; rerun under same inputs yields equivalent journal semantics.

Summary: Deliver first-paid-call guided remediation flow
Description: Implement guided first-paid-call flow that maps failed steps to explicit remediation actions.
Acceptance Criteria: At least 90% of scripted first-paid-call failures produce actionable remediation guidance; successful flow reaches verified payment and settlement evidence.

Summary: Enforce onboarding metrics and SLO dashboards
Description: Expose onboarding metrics and tie p50/p95 activation SLOs to alerting thresholds.
Acceptance Criteria: Metrics endpoint reports onboarding funnel stages; SLO alerts trigger for threshold breaches; weekly trend report auto-generated.

Summary: Ship design-partner onboarding runbook
Description: Create an operator runbook for onboarding design partners, including escalation and rollback actions.
Acceptance Criteria: Runbook is used in at least one dry-run with full checklist completion; escalation contacts and SLAs are explicit and tested.

Summary: Harden wallet bootstrap and funding flows
Description: Stabilize wallet bootstrap and funding endpoints across hosted onboarding and kernel-level x402 wallet setup.
Acceptance Criteria: Wallet bootstrap and funding pass success/failure matrix; no partial funded state without explicit status evidence; retries are idempotent.

Summary: Add authorize-payment idempotency matrix
Description: Cover duplicate, reordered, and replayed authorization attempts with deterministic idempotent outcomes.
Acceptance Criteria: Authorization replay matrix passes with no double-authorization; duplicate requests return deterministic prior result binding.

Summary: Harden verify/reversal/dispute handoff
Description: Ensure verify and reversal outcomes align with dispute opening and settlement state transitions.
Acceptance Criteria: Verify/reversal/dispute transitions are replay-safe and append-only; inconsistent transitions fail with stable reject codes.

Summary: Set settlement replay and explainability latency budgets
Description: Measure and enforce p95 latency budgets for settlement replay/evaluate/explainability retrieval paths.
Acceptance Criteria: Latency budgets are documented and monitored; budget regressions trigger alerts and release gate warnings.

Summary: Implement degraded payment-rail fail-closed mode
Description: Add explicit degraded mode behavior for partial rail outages that preserves fund safety and evidence completeness.
Acceptance Criteria: Rail outage simulations deny unsafe actions, preserve existing funds, and emit deterministic outage reason codes.

Summary: Freeze intent contract schema and fixtures
Description: Lock intent contract shape and canonical hashing fixtures for beta compatibility.
Acceptance Criteria: Intent schema and fixtures are versioned; canonical hashes are stable across repeated runs; compatibility checks run in CI.

Summary: Enforce strict request binding for side-effect routes
Description: Require strict request hash/path/method binding on all side-effecting routes and remove permissive legacy edges.
Acceptance Criteria: Side-effect requests without valid strict binding are rejected deterministically; read-only and compute paths preserve backward compatibility.

Summary: Require replay-pack verification on paid runs
Description: Bind paid-run completion to replay-pack generation and verification APIs for auditability.
Acceptance Criteria: Every paid run has replay-pack and verification status retrievable by API; missing replay-pack blocks final success verdict.

Summary: Add deterministic artifact hash soak gate
Description: Run repeated export/replay cycles and assert stable artifact hashes and decision fingerprints.
Acceptance Criteria: Equivalent runs produce stable semantic hashes across soak iterations; drift triggers gate failure with artifact diff pointers.

Summary: Add evidence-mismatch fail-closed regression suite
Description: Prove settlement/dispute operations fail closed when evidence hash bindings are missing or mismatched.
Acceptance Criteria: Regression suite blocks merges on evidence mismatch acceptance; reject codes remain stable for integration consumers.

Summary: Deploy split API/worker/maintenance topology
Description: Run API, workers, and maintenance roles as independent processes with explicit startup and readiness checks.
Acceptance Criteria: Topology deploy is documented and repeatable; each role has independent health signal; worker/maintenance outages do not hide API health state.

Summary: Deepen health/readiness/metrics checks
Description: Add component-level readiness checks and standardized metrics labels for DB, queues, and external dependencies.
Acceptance Criteria: Readiness reports component detail with stable schema; alerting can isolate component failures without ambiguous status.

Summary: Add queue retry and dead-letter guardrails
Description: Implement bounded retries, dead-letter capture, and alerting for queue-backed work paths.
Acceptance Criteria: Failed jobs move to dead-letter with evidence context; retry limits prevent infinite loops; alerts fire on dead-letter growth.

Summary: Run synthetic paid-flow canaries
Description: Schedule synthetic end-to-end canaries covering onboarding, paid call, settlement, and evidence retrieval.
Acceptance Criteria: Canary runs at fixed cadence with signed reports; any hard-step failure pages on-call and blocks promotion.

Summary: Run rollback drill and incident packet signing
Description: Perform rollback rehearsal and produce signed incident packet proving recovery path viability.
Acceptance Criteria: Rollback drill completes within defined window; incident packet includes timeline, checks, and signed artifact hashes.

Summary: Ship dispute and settlement ops control surface
Description: Provide operator views/API bundles for active disputes, settlement anomalies, and evidence drill-down.
Acceptance Criteria: Ops surface supports triage without direct DB access; every action links to immutable evidence references.

Summary: Define incident severity model and on-call routing
Description: Formalize severity classes, escalation times, and routing policies for reliability, payment, and security incidents.
Acceptance Criteria: Severity model is documented and tested in tabletop drill; alert routing confirms correct responder assignment.

Summary: Publish status page and RCA templates
Description: Implement public/internal status communication templates and enforce RCA completion workflow.
Acceptance Criteria: Status updates follow defined cadence; RCA template includes root cause, corrective actions, and verification links.

Summary: Deliver abuse and fraud triage playbook
Description: Define abuse intake, fraud escalation, and tenant containment actions with evidence preservation requirements.
Acceptance Criteria: Abuse/fraud playbook covers detection, containment, escalation, and recovery; drill validates timing and evidence capture.

Summary: Unify beta package distribution surface
Description: Align CLI, SDK, provider-kit, and quickstart docs into a single externally consistent beta install path.
Acceptance Criteria: External team can install and run beta flow using one quickstart path; package/version matrix is internally consistent.

Summary: Automate design-partner launch packets
Description: Generate tenant-specific launch packets with onboarding status, gates, and support contacts.
Acceptance Criteria: Launch packet generation is automated and auditable; packet includes onboarding proof, gate status, and escalation routes.

Summary: Implement beta promotion gate bundle
Description: Create a single beta promotion gate command bundle that aggregates onboarding, install, reliability, and evidence gates.
Acceptance Criteria: Promotion command emits machine-readable report with blocking issues; beta release is blocked unless all required gates pass.

Summary: Set public beta pricing and quota guardrails
Description: Define beta usage limits, spend caps, and automatic throttles to bound financial and abuse risk.
Acceptance Criteria: Quota and spend controls enforce configured limits; over-limit flows return deterministic responses and preserve settlement invariants.

Summary: Stand up beta activation funnel and weekly ops review
Description: Implement activation dashboard and weekly review cadence for onboarding, reliability, and economic health metrics.
Acceptance Criteria: Weekly review packet auto-generates with trend deltas; owners and follow-ups are tracked per metric regression.
